steps.title: Set up the x-intercept equation
steps.content:
To find where the graph crosses the x-axis, set $y = 0$:
$$0 = 9\left(\frac{a}{6}\right)^{x+c} - b$$
Rearranging:
$$9\left(\frac{a}{6}\right)^{x+c} = b$$

steps.title: Analyze the base and monotonicity
steps.content: Since $a \gt 6$, the base satisfies $\frac{a}{6} \gt 1$. When an exponential function has a base greater than 1, it is strictly increasing. Therefore, $f(x) = 9\left(\frac{a}{6}\right)^{x+c}$ is strictly increasing in $x$.

steps.title: Determine the range and count intersections
steps.content:
As $x$ varies over all real numbers:
  • As $x \to -\infty$: $\left(\frac{a}{6}\right)^{x+c} \to 0$, so $f(x) \to 0^+$
  • As $x \to \infty$: $\left(\frac{a}{6}\right)^{x+c} \to \infty$, so $f(x) \to \infty$
The function is continuous and strictly increasing with range $(0, \infty)$. Since $b \gt 0$ is a single positive value, the strictly increasing function $f(x)$ equals $b$ at exactly one point. That confirms B is correct.
